title: Triage a Monday Morning Inbox
tagline: Prioritise 10 support tickets from an e-commerce backlog.
scenario: You are a junior support agent at a South African online retailer (similar to Takealot). It is Monday morning after a busy Black Friday weekend. You have a backlog of 1,000 tickets. Your team lead gives you a sample of 10 tickets and asks you to triage them.
brief: Review the 10 tickets. Categorise each by issue type (e.g., Delivery, Payment, Return) and assign a priority (Low, Medium, High, Urgent). Write a 1-sentence rationale for each priority assignment.
deliverables: A triage log for 10 simulated tickets (provided in the prompt context) showing Category, Priority, and Rationale
Identification of the 2 tickets that require immediate escalation to a manager
A brief explanation of your overall triage strategy
submissionGuidance: Urgent means business impact or severe customer distress (e.g., a payment went through 3 times). A late delivery is frustrating, but usually Medium or High, not Urgent. Note: Invent 10 realistic ticket scenarios to triage.

rubric.label: Ticket Categorisation
rubric.description: Are the tickets correctly categorised by issue type?

rubric.label: Prioritisation Judgment
rubric.description: Is the priority level appropriate for the business impact?

rubric.label: Clear Rationale
rubric.description: Is the reasoning for each priority clear and logical?
